findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.audit.render-template-string
findings.description: Found a template created with string formatting. This is susceptible to server-side template injection and cross-site scripting attacks.
cwe_ids: CWE-96: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Statically Saved Code ('Static Code Injection')
findings.code_excerpt:
return render_template_string("""

Welcome, {{ username }}!

Logout

""", username=username)

Welcome, {{ username }}!

Logout

""", username=username)

findings.description: Found a template created with string formatting. This is susceptible to server-side template injection and cross-site scripting attacks.
cwe_ids: CWE-96: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Statically Saved Code ('Static Code Injection')
findings.code_excerpt:

Login

{% if error %}

{{ error }}

{% endif %}

Username:

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.lang.security.audit.md5-used-as-password
findings.description: It looks like MD5 is used as a password hash. MD5 is not considered a secure password hash because it can be cracked by an attacker in a short amount of time. Use a suitable password hashing function such as bcrypt. You can use the `bcrypt` node.js package.
cwe_ids: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.lang.security.audit.md5-used-as-password
findings.description: It looks like MD5 is used as a password hash. MD5 is not considered a secure password hash because it can be cracked by an attacker in a short amount of time. Use a suitable password hashing function such as bcrypt. You can use the `bcrypt` node.js package.
cwe_ids: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.audit.xss.direct-response-write
findings.description: Detected directly writing to a Response object from user-defined input. This bypasses any HTML escaping and may expose your application to a Cross-Site-scripting (XSS) vulnerability. Instead, use 'resp.render()' to render safely escaped HTML.
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.audit.dangerous-subprocess-use-audit

Audit the use of this call to ensure it is not controllable by an external resource. You may consider using 'shlex.escape()'.
cwe_ids: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.audit.dangerous-subprocess-use-tainted-env-args

cwe_ids: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.deserialization.avoid-dill
findings.description: Avoid using `dill`, which uses `pickle`, which is known to lead to code execution vulnerabilities. When unpickling, the serialized data could be manipulated to run arbitrary code. Instead, consider serializing the relevant data as JSON or a similar text-based serialization format.
owasp_categories: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.jwt.security.unverified-jwt-decode
findings.description: Detected JWT token decoded with 'verify=False'. This bypasses any integrity checks for the token which means the token could be tampered with by malicious actors. Ensure that the JWT token is verified.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.generic.secrets.security.detected-jwt-token
findings.description: JWT token detected
cwe_ids: CWE-321: Use of Hard-coded Cryptographic Key

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.generic.secrets.gitleaks.generic-api-key
findings.description: A gitleaks generic-api-key was detected which attempts to identify hard-coded credentials.  It is not recommended to store credentials in source-code, as this risks secrets being leaked  and used by either an internal or external malicious adversary. It is recommended to use  environment variables to securely provide credentials or retrieve credentials from a  secure vault or HSM (Hardware Security Module). This rule can introduce a lot of false positives,  it is not recommended to be used in PR comments.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.open-redirect
findings.description: Data from request is passed to redirect(). This is an open redirect and could be exploited. Consider using 'url_for()' to generate links to known locations. If you must use a URL to unknown pages, consider using 'urlparse()' or similar and checking if the 'netloc' property is the same as your site's host name. See the references for more information.
cwe_ids: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

Welcome, {name}!

Welcome, {name}!

Hope you have an amazing day.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.django.security.injection.raw-html-format
findings.description: Detected user input flowing into a manually constructed HTML string. You may be accidentally bypassing secure methods of rendering HTML by manually constructing HTML and this could create a cross-site scripting vulnerability, which could let attackers steal sensitive user data. To be sure this is safe, check that the HTML is rendered safely. Otherwise, use templates (`django.shortcuts.render`) which will safely render HTML instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
findings.code_excerpt:
greeting = f"

Welcome, {name}!

Hope you have an amazing day.

"

tool_specific.lines:
greeting = f"

Welcome, {name}!

Hope you have an amazing day.

"

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.injection.raw-html-format
findings.description: Detected user input flowing into a manually constructed HTML string. You may be accidentally bypassing secure methods of rendering HTML by manually constructing HTML and this could create a cross-site scripting vulnerability, which could let attackers steal sensitive user data. To be sure this is safe, check that the HTML is rendered safely. Otherwise, use templates (`flask.render_template`) which will safely render HTML instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

findings.description: Found a template created with string formatting. This is susceptible to server-side template injection and cross-site scripting attacks.
cwe_ids: CWE-96: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Statically Saved Code ('Static Code Injection')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.audit.debug-enabled
findings.description: Detected Flask app with debug=True. Do not deploy to production with this flag enabled as it will leak sensitive information. Instead, consider using Flask configuration variables or setting 'debug' using system environment variables.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.use-defused-xml
findings.description: The Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml` instead of `xml` because the native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service.
cwe_ids: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.use-defused-xml-parse
findings.description: The native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks.  These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service. Do not use this library to parse untrusted input. Instead  the Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml`.
cwe_ids: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.audit.express-libxml-vm-noent

cwe_ids: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference
